question_text: Which of the following best explains why incandescent light bulbs have much lower efficiency than LED bulbs?

options.label: A
options.text: LED bulbs use less electrical current than incandescent bulbs

options.label: B
options.text: Incandescent bulbs convert most of their electrical energy into heat rather than visible light

options.label: C
options.text: LED bulbs are made from materials that absorb electricity more efficiently

options.label: D
options.text: Incandescent bulbs have a higher voltage requirement than LED bulbs

explanation: Incandescent bulbs work by heating a filament until it glows, so about 90% of the energy becomes heat and only about 10% becomes visible light. LEDs convert electricity directly into light through a different process, wasting much less energy as heat.
